steps.title: Substitute x = 3 into the function
steps.content:
We need to find $f(3)$, which means we substitute $x = 3$ into $f(x) = \frac{1}{6x}$:

$$f(3) = \frac{1}{6 \cdot 3}$$

steps.title: Simplify the denominator
steps.content:
Multiply the denominator: $6 \cdot 3 = 18$, so:

$$f(3) = \frac{1}{18}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
